canonical_definition: In the surveyed literature, 'Audio question answering' is characterized as the behavior of generating answers to natural language questions about audio content. The single available definition specifies that this task is based on both the audio input and accompanying text. The primary way this behavior is operationalized and measured in the provided sources is through the ClothoAQA dataset. One paper describes this dataset as comprising audio samples paired with "crowdsourced questions and corresponding answers" ("CalligraphicOCRforChinese Calligraphy Recognition"). Performance on the task is assessed using accuracy, with one study reporting specific accuracy scores for a model.
papers: CMedCalc-Bench: A Fine-Grained Benchmark forChinese Medical Calculations inLLM
Improving Instruct Models for Free: A Study on Partial Adaptation

definitions.text: Generating answers to natural language questions about audio content based on both audio input and accompanying text.

relationships.paper: Audio Flamingo 2: An Audio-Language Model with Long-Audio Understanding and Expert Reasoning Abilities
